tier: practicable (mid=P50 saline onshore); alt technical (USGS TASR ~59% of 3,000 Gt national); alt aggregated CSRC-lineage (whole-USA figure, not basin-specific)
source: Teletzke et al. 2018 (GHGT-14); USGS National Assessment 2013 (share of national total)
notes: Dominant US basin in every tier: 72% of Teletzke practicable (366 of 506 Gt), ~59% of USGS technical (~1,770 of 3,000 Gt). High=8,062 is the CSRC whole-country aggregated figure, not basin-attributed -- included only as an upper anchor, do not treat as basin-specific.

source: Teletzke et al. 2018 (GHGT-14) via PMC10569028 secondary citation
notes: Second-largest US practicable basin after Gulf Coast.

source: Teletzke et al. 2018 (GHGT-14)

source: Teletzke et al. 2018 (GHGT-14)

source: USGS Geologic framework for the National Assessment, Chapter P

source: USGS 'Geologic framework for the national assessment of carbon dioxide storage resources: Alaska North Slope and Kandik Basin, Alaska'

tier: mixed: mid=theoretical/technical (WCSB only); high=national theoretical (51 basins, includes non-WCSB); low=CSRC aggregated (national, stricter maturity)

notes: WCSB (mid=385) subsumes the Canadian portion of the Williston Basin -- do not add a separate Williston(Canada) row on top of this. National 877 Gt figure (high) spans ALL Canadian basins, not just WCSB, and is unverified against Bachu primary literature; treat as upper bound only.

source: CSRC Cycle 4 (51.2) / Cycle 5 (120.66)

basin: Norwegian North Sea / Norwegian Sea Basin

tier: aggregated (CSRC SRMS Cycle 4, HRV+HUN+AUT+SVK summed); high includes CO2StoP theoretical for AUT
source: CSRC Cycle 4: HRV 4.897 + HUN 10.828 + AUT 0.146 + SVK 1.8 = 17.67 (recompute: mid uses HRV+HUN=15.73 as the basin's dominant share; Vienna Basin sub-portion AUT+SVK ~1.95); GEUS 2021 (AUT CO2StoP theoretical 20 Gt)
notes: Composite entry spanning multiple countries' CSRC totals. Croatia and Hungary sit on the main Pannonian Basin; Austria and Slovakia's portions are more specifically the Vienna Basin sub-basin (a Pannonian Basin System component) -- consider splitting into two polygon rows (Pannonian Basin proper: HRV+HUN; Vienna Basin: AUT+SVK) if the basin polygon layer distinguishes them. Austria shows the largest CSRC-vs-CO2StoP ratio in the European dataset (~140x), implying its mature/discovered resource base is minuscule relative to theoretical saline potential.

source: CSRC Cycle 4 (2024)

tier: n/a -- parent geologic province, not independently quantified
source: n/a

source: Evaluation of geological CO2 storage potential in Saudi Arabian sedimentary basins, ScienceDirect S0012825223002283
notes: Saudi Arabia's national 432-446 Gt effective-capacity total spans several basins (Eastern Arabian Basin, Interior Homocline-Central Arch, Rub al Khali); this row isolates the specific 40-318 Gt Rub al Khali sub-range cited for the basin, roughly a third of the national figure at midpoint. UAE's CSRC figure (16.7 Gt, saline, 5.91 project-specified) is entirely attributed to its Rub al Khali portion -- do not double-count against this basin-level range if summing UAE's country total separately.

tier: effective (academic, residual national total minus Rub al Khali)
source: derived: Saudi national effective total (~432-446 Gt, S0012825223002283) minus Rub al Khali sub-range (~150 Gt mid) -- NOT independently basin-attributed in the source
notes: Home of the Ghawar super-giant field and Unayzah Formation, the world's largest conventional oil reservoir system and a leading CO2-EOR/storage target; the ~9 million tpa Jubail CCS hub (Aramco/Linde/SLB, 2027 startup) targets this basin. Mid value is a derived residual, not a directly cited basin figure -- treat as low confidence.

source: CSRC Cycle 5 (2025)
notes: CSRC figure almost certainly understates true basin potential (Oman is a large, geologically complex salt basin); flagged as likely under-assessed at CSRC's maturity standard rather than genuinely low-capacity.

tier: aggregated (CSRC SRMS Cycle 5) / effective (academic, CSLF/USDOE method)

notes: Strong cross-validation: CSRC (582.88 Gt) and independent academic effective-capacity estimate (583 Gt) agree almost exactly for the Kazakh sector -- one of the best-corroborated figures in this dataset. The basin extends into Russia (Astrakhan/Volgograd oblasts) where no separate assessment was found; Russia is otherwise a major national data gap (absent from CSRC entirely, no citable national total).

Top data-gap priority for a major-emitter country.

tier: unassessed at Gt scale in this pass; pre-salt EOR caveat applies

Brazil's CSRC national total (2.47 Gt) is 100% depleted O&G fields and excludes Santos' large saline aquifer prospectivity.
